REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. WARREN CHEUNG IS NOT THE PROPER PLAINTIFF BECAUSE HE WAS NOT THE OWNER OF THE PROPERTY AT THE TIME OF THE FORECLOSURE SALE. THE QUITCLAIM DEED THAT PURPORTEDLY TRANSFERRED THE PROPERTY TO PLAINTIFF'S COMPANY WAS RECORDED ON DECEMBER 19, 2024 AT 9:04 AM, APPROXIMATELY ONE HOUR AFTER THE FORECLOSURE WAS DEEMED "FINAL" AND "PERFECTED." (SEE CAL. CIV. CODE 2924H(C)). THE COURT DECLINES TO ADDRESS THE MERITS OF EACH CAUSE OF ACTION WITHOUT THE PROPER PARTIES, BUT ENCOURAGES PLAINTIFF TO ADDRESS IN GOOD FAITH THE ISSUES RAISED BY THE DEMURRER, INCLUDING, BUT NOT LIMITED TO: THE BANKRUPTCY COURT'S DECEMBER 4, 2024 ORDER AUTHORING A FORECLOSURE SALE, THE FAILURE TO ALLEGE TENDER, THE LACK OF PARTICULARITY IN CAUSES OF ACTION 1-3, AND THE FACT THAT CAUSES OF ACTION 5 AND 6 ARE LABELED REMEDIES RATHER THAN CAUSES OF ACTION. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). JUDGE: SAMUEL K. FENG; CLERK: KENNETH B. HUNT; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

DECLARATION OF WARREN SHUNG CHEUNG IN SUPPORT OF PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CHEUNG, WARREN SHUNG

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITH FINAL LEAVE TO AMEND. FIRST, THE COURT NOTES THAT THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT IS BEING BROUGHT BY WARREN SHUNG CHEUNG IN HIS REPRESENTATIVE CAPACITY AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE. (TAC, PARA. 1.) THE COURT HAS PREVIOUSLY GIVEN PLAINTIFF LEAVE TO AMEND TO EITHER PROPERLY ALLEGE STANDING OR SUBSTITUTE THE APPROPRIATE PLAINTIFF. IN THE COURT'S JANUARY 14, 2026 RULING ON DEMURRER TO THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT, THE COURT NOTED THAT IT WAS TAKING JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE NOTICE OF APPEAL FILED ON OCTOBER 20, 2025 BY TIFFANY CHOU, AS TRUSTEE IN CASE CUD-25-677947, WHICH WAS FILED MONTHS AFTER THE PURPOSED APPOINTMENT OF PLAINTIFF AS SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE. THE THIRD AMENDED COMPLAINT STILL ALLEGES THAT MR. CHUENG WAS APPOINTED SUCCESSOR TRUSTEE IN JUNE 2025. FOR THE SECOND TIME, THE COURT TAKES JUDICIAL NOTICE OF THE NOTICE OF APPEAL FILED ON OCTOBER 20, 2025 BY TIFFANY CHOU, AS TRUSTEE IN CASE CUD-25-677947. PLAINTIFF MUST RECONCILE THE TIMELINE. SETTING ASIDE THE ISSUE OF STANDING, THE DEMURRER AS TO ALL CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO IDENTIFY THE STATUTE(S) DEFENDANT PURPORTEDLY VIOLATED AND THE UNDERLYING FACTS DESCRIBING HOW THE VIOLATION(S) OCCURRED, NOR HAS PLAINTIFF ALLEGED TENDER. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312(B). VISITING JUDGE: DENNIS HAYASHI; CLERK: K. VALDES; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2026 Continued To Sep-16-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

Defendants' Demurrer to the Third Amended Complaint

is SUSTAINED with final leave to amend. First, the Court notes that the Third Amended Complaint is being brought by Warren Shung Cheung in his representative capacity as Successor Trustee. (TAC, para. 1.) The Court has previously given Plaintiff leave to amend to either properly allege standing or substitute the appropriate Plaintiff. In the Court's January 14, 2026 ruling on demurrer to the Second Amended Complaint, the Court noted that it was taking judicial notice of the Notice of Appeal filed on October 20, 2025 by Tiffany Chou, as Trustee in case CUD-25-677947, which was filed months after the purposed appointment of Plaintiff as Successor Trustee. The Third Amended Complaint still alleges that Mr. Chueng was appointed successor trustee in June 2025. For the second time, the Court takes judicial notice of the Notice of Appeal filed on October 20, 2025 by Tiffany Chou, as Trustee in case CUD-25-677947. Plaintiff must reconcile the timeline.
Setting aside the issue of standing, the demurrer as to all causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to identify the statute(s) Defendant purportedly violated and the underlying facts describing how the violation(s) occurred, nor has Plaintiff alleged tender.

Prevailing Party to submit proposed order that is in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b).

Visiting Judge: Dennis Hayashi; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

is OFF CALENDAR. Amended Demurrer on file.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

Amended DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-14-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances
Plaintiff Warren Shung Cheung appeared in pro per.
Reilly Wilkinson, Esq. representing Defendant Douglas Sykes.

Plaintiff declines the assistance of a Cantonese speaking interpreter.

Hearing held.

Plaintiff does not contest the tentative ruling.
Defendant argues as to the tentative ruling and provides proof.
Plaintiff requests for 2 weeks.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS SYKES AND ENTRA DEFAULT SOLUTIONS LLC'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT

is SUSTAINED with leave to amend to either properly allege standing or substitute the appropriate Plaintiff. The Court takes judicial notice of the Notice of Appeal filed on October 20, 2025 by Tiffany Chou, as Trustee in case CUD-25-677947. This filing was made months after the purported appointment of Ms. Cheung as Successor Trustee (See Appointment of Successor Trustee attached as exhibit to the Second Amended Complaint).

Plaintiff's Counsel is to prepare an order for review and signature of the court.

Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: K. Valdes; Not Reported. (D501)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Continued To Nov-26-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion Of Substitution Of Party
Off Calendar Sep-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff's Motion of Substitution of Party is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient Notice pursuant to CCP 1005(b).

Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-02-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Warren Cheung is not the proper Plaintiff because he was not the owner of the property at the time of the foreclosure sale. The quitclaim deed that purportedly transferred the property to Plaintiff's company was recorded on December 19, 2024 at 9:04 am, approximately one hour after the foreclosure was deemed "final" and "perfected." (See Cal. Civ. Code 2924h(c)). The Court declines to address the merits of each cause of action without the proper parties, but encourages Plaintiff to address in good faith the issues raised by the demurrer, including, but not limited to: the Bankruptcy Court's December 4, 2024 order authoring a foreclosure sale, the failure to allege tender, the lack of particularity in causes of action 1-3, and the fact that causes of action 5 and 6 are labeled remedies rather than causes of action.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Samuel K. Feng; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

(Moving papers received.) Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported (501/CFH)

MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF WARREN CHEUNG Notice Of Motion To Stay Unlawful Detainer Proceedings is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice under CCP section 1005(b). The motion must be filed and served 16 court days before the hearing, plus 5 days for service by mail. Additionally, to the extent Plaintiff wishes to re-file a motion to stay the UD case, Plaintiff must file said motion in the UD case.

Judge: Dennis W. Hayashi ; Clerk: L. Young ; Not Reported. (D501)
